title: eryngium aquifolium subsp. caespitiferum
intro: Eryngium aquifolium subsp. caespitiferum, also known as the creeping sea holly, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Apiaceae, characterized by its blue-green foliage and blue or white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Eryngium aquifolium subsp. caespitiferum has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
